title: 001 — Missing — Persons — Reyes
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_001_missing_persons_reyes
SOURCE_TYPE: official_report
AUTHOR: Ofc. Linda Hauser, Ashford PD
RECIPIENT: Ashford PD case file
TIMESTAMP: 1988-07-14T16:20:00
LOCATION: Ashford Police Station
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.82

TEXT:
Missing persons report filed by Elena Reyes regarding her son Daniel Reyes, 19, of 1814 Pierce Ave. Last seen by family on the morning of July 12, 1988. Subject did not return home that night. Mother became concerned on the 13th when subject failed to pick up younger sister from summer school. Subject is 5'8", brown hair, brown eyes, was wearing a gray t-shirt and jeans. Works part-time at Klein's Supermarket. No history of running away. No known disputes. Mother states subject typically spent evenings at Empire Arcade on Mercer Street. Inquiry to be opened.

title: 002 — Arcade — Cashier — Statement
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_002_arcade_cashier_statement
SOURCE_TYPE: witness_statement
AUTHOR: Marcus Lin, cashier (Empire Arcade)
RECIPIENT: Ofc. Linda Hauser
TIMESTAMP: 1988-07-15T11:00:00
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.55

TEXT:
Daniel was a regular. He'd been on the pinball machines most of the evening of the 12th — the Bally one by the back wall. Around ten-thirty or so, a man came in. Older guy, tall, glasses, denim jacket. He watched Daniel play for a while, didn't put any tokens down himself. Then he went over and said something to Daniel at the change machine. I couldn't hear over the noise. Few minutes later they walked out together. I'd never seen the guy before. He didn't look like he belonged in an arcade. Time? Maybe ten-thirty. Could've been later. The clock by the door has been wrong for months — runs slow.

title: 003 — Smiths — Tap — Statement
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_003_smiths_tap_statement
SOURCE_TYPE: witness_statement
AUTHOR: Frank Brennan, bartender (Smith's Tap)
RECIPIENT: Det. Hassan Reilly
TIMESTAMP: 1988-07-17T13:45:00
LOCATION: Smith's Tap, 218 Mercer St.
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.62

TEXT:
Yeah, I remember the order because I had to get the bucket of ice from the back. Rum and coke, extra ice. Guy was specific about it. Soft voice, didn't have to raise it over the jukebox even though I almost couldn't hear him. Maybe eleven-thirty, quarter to twelve? Younger guy with him was quiet. Not drunk, not exactly, but slow — like he was already tired. Paid in cash, both drinks, and a five for me. I couldn't tell you what the older guy looked like to save my life. Average. Glasses maybe. The kind of face you forget by the time he's out the door.

title: 004 — Coroner — Report — Reyes
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_004_coroner_report_reyes
SOURCE_TYPE: medical_report
AUTHOR: Dr. Edward Mariani, Ashford County Coroner
RECIPIENT: Ashford PD
TIMESTAMP: 1988-08-05T09:30:00
LOCATION: County Morgue
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.86

TEXT:
Subject identified via dental records as Daniel Reyes. Body recovered August 3 along Norfolk Southern right-of-way, mile 142.6. Decomposition consistent with approximately three weeks post-mortem; consistent with disappearance date of July 12-13. Toxicology positive for barbiturate metabolites, concentration consistent with overdose. No blunt force trauma. No sharp force trauma. No ligature marks. Cause of death: probable acute barbiturate intoxication. Manner: undetermined; circumstances suggestive of administration by another party. Lividity patterns inconsistent with recovery position — body was moved post-mortem. Insect activity at recovery site does not match a body that decomposed in situ for the full interval.

title: 005 — Track — Inspector — Note
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_005_track_inspector_note
SOURCE_TYPE: incident_note
AUTHOR: Howard Bell, Norfolk Southern track inspector
RECIPIENT: NS dispatch / forwarded to Ashford PD
TIMESTAMP: 1988-08-03T14:12:00
LOCATION: NS mile 142.6, west of Ashford yards
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.74

TEXT:
Smelled it before I saw it. Body about twenty feet off the rail, in the weeds. Didn't go closer once I was sure. Called it in to dispatch. While I was waiting I noticed a camera case in the brush, maybe ten feet from the body — black, Polaroid type, the kind with the strap. Empty. No camera inside, no film. Didn't pick it up. Told the deputy when he arrived. I've walked that stretch twice a week for nine years and that case wasn't there last Tuesday.

title: 006 — Letter — Elena — To — Sister
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_006_letter_elena_to_sister
SOURCE_TYPE: private_letter
AUTHOR: Elena Reyes
RECIPIENT: Carmen Reyes-Ortiz
TIMESTAMP: 1988-09-04T20:00:00
LOCATION: Reyes home, Ashford
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.78

TEXT:
Carmen,
I keep thinking about the camera. He had been saving for one all spring — a real one, not the disposable kind. He asked me for forty dollars in June and I told him I didn't have it. I keep thinking, if I had given him the forty, would he have stayed home that night to look at catalogs instead of going to the arcade. The detective asked if Danny had any new friends. He didn't, not that I knew. He hadn't been to the arcade for almost two months in the spring — he was working extra shifts — and then he started going again in June. I wonder now if someone had been waiting for him to come back.
Elena

title: 007 — Missing — Persons — Webb
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_007_missing_persons_webb
SOURCE_TYPE: official_report
AUTHOR: Det. Hassan Reilly, Ashford PD
RECIPIENT: Ashford PD case file
TIMESTAMP: 1989-03-17T10:15:00
LOCATION: Ashford Police Station
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.81

TEXT:
Missing persons report regarding Marcus Webb, 22, Black male, 6'1", recently of 904 Halsted St., Apt 2. Reported by sister Cheryl Webb of Lexington, KY, who states last phone contact was March 8 evening. Subject relocated from Lexington in November 1988 for employment at Hartford Hotel laundry, night shift. Report filed nine days after last contact due to family assumption that subject had simply not called. Subject has no Ashford-area family. Coworker confirms subject did not show for March 9 or any subsequent shift. Employer assumed subject had quit without notice.

title: 008 — Lantern — Bartender — Statement
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_008_lantern_bartender_statement
SOURCE_TYPE: witness_statement
AUTHOR: Joanne Markevich, bartender (The Lantern)
RECIPIENT: Det. Hassan Reilly
TIMESTAMP: 1989-03-19T17:00:00
LOCATION: The Lantern, 1145 Halsted St.
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.71

TEXT:
Marcus had been coming in two or three nights a week since the new year. Quiet kid. Nursed a beer. Didn't bother anybody. The night of the eighth he was at the end of the bar talking to a regular — well, not really a regular, but a guy who'd been in maybe five or six times since fall. Tall, glasses, soft voice. He always ordered the same thing: rum and coke, extra ice, drank it slow. Paid cash both times I served him that night. They left together a little before one. I remember because I was about to call last call. I never caught the guy's name. He wasn't friendly enough to introduce himself, but he wasn't unfriendly either. He just wasn't there for the company.

title: 009 — Culvert — Discovery — Report
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_009_culvert_discovery_report
SOURCE_TYPE: official_report
AUTHOR: Deputy J. Tarrant, Ashford County Sheriff
RECIPIENT: Sheriff's case file / Ashford PD
TIMESTAMP: 1989-03-22T15:40:00
LOCATION: Drainage culvert, Route 14, mile 8.3
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.83

TEXT:
Body of an adult Black male recovered from drainage culvert running under Route 14, approximately 400 yards from the rail spur servicing the disused Pruitt grain elevator. Reported by motorist Sandra Vincelli who pulled over after seeing what she initially thought was a discarded mattress in the ditch. Body partially submerged in standing water. No wallet, no identification, no jewelry. Partially clothed; some garments missing. Recovered to morgue for identification. Fingerprints subsequently matched to Marcus Webb.

title: 010 — Toxicology — Webb
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_010_toxicology_webb
SOURCE_TYPE: medical_report
AUTHOR: Dr. Edward Mariani
RECIPIENT: Ashford County Sheriff / Ashford PD
TIMESTAMP: 1989-03-25T11:20:00
LOCATION: County Morgue
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.86

TEXT:
Subject: Marcus Webb. Sedative metabolites present in liver tissue — barbiturate class, profile consistent with secobarbital or a closely related compound. Concentration suggests an overdose dose, though degradation from water exposure precludes precision. No defensive wounds. No ligature marks. No blunt force trauma. Time of death estimate hampered by submersion: best estimate 10-14 days prior to recovery, placing death in the window of March 8-12, 1989, consistent with disappearance date. Manner of death: undetermined; circumstances strongly suggestive of administration by another party.

title: 011 — Letter — Cheryl — Webb
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_011_letter_cheryl_webb
SOURCE_TYPE: private_letter
AUTHOR: Cheryl Webb
RECIPIENT: Det. Hassan Reilly
TIMESTAMP: 1989-04-02T19:30:00
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.69

TEXT:
Detective Reilly,
I keep thinking of the call. It was Wednesday the first of March, week before he went missing. He told me a guy had offered him fifty dollars to "pose for some pictures." He was laughing about it. I asked if it was a creep thing and he said no, the guy seemed normal, said he did portraits. Marcus said he wasn't going to do it but he was thinking about whether he could get more money. I didn't ask where he'd met the guy. I wish I had. He also said he was thinking about leaving Ashford in the summer. The work was hard and he hadn't made any friends. I should have made him come home in February when he asked about the bus fare.
Cheryl

title: 012 — Hotel — Coworker — Statement
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_012_hotel_coworker_statement
SOURCE_TYPE: witness_statement
AUTHOR: Robin DiCaro, laundry attendant (Hartford Hotel)
RECIPIENT: Det. Hassan Reilly
TIMESTAMP: 1989-03-28T22:00:00
LOCATION: Hartford Hotel staff entrance
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.58

TEXT:
Marcus told me once that some guy had come up to him on his smoke break and asked if he did any modeling. He thought it was creepy but he was kind of flattered too. I never saw the guy myself — Marcus pointed at the alley but the guy was already gone by then. When was it? I don't know exactly. Two weeks before he disappeared? Three? Maybe more. We didn't talk about it again. Marcus was the kind who'd shrug something like that off and not mention it twice.

title: 013 — Halloran — Scene — Report
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_013_halloran_scene_report
SOURCE_TYPE: official_report
AUTHOR: Det. Mariah Sweeney, Ashford PD
RECIPIENT: Ashford PD case file
TIMESTAMP: 1989-12-18T08:50:00
LOCATION: Apt 3C, 412 Wendell St.
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.84

TEXT:
Responded to call at 06:42, 18 December 1989. Apartment 3C, 412 Wendell. Decedent: Eric Halloran, 24, white male. Discovered by roommate Patrick Vail at approximately 06:20. Body supine in bed. Apparent cause of death ligature strangulation; electrical cord from bedside lamp wrapped twice around the neck. Lamp lying broken on the floor on the south side of the bed, base shattered. Defensive injuries on both forearms, abrasions consistent with grabbing or being grabbed. Bruising at the throat under the ligature includes thumb-shaped marks suggesting manual pressure preceded the ligature. Wallet on dresser contains $120 cash and credit cards; untouched. No drawers disturbed. Front door not forced; deadbolt unlocked from the inside. Rear window closed and locked. No defensive weapons recovered.

title: 014 — Vail — First — Statement
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_014_vail_first_statement
SOURCE_TYPE: witness_statement
AUTHOR: Patrick Vail
RECIPIENT: Det. Mariah Sweeney
TIMESTAMP: 1989-12-18T11:30:00
LOCATION: Ashford Police Station
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.45

TEXT:
We were drinking. Yeah, we'd been fighting. The same thing. He wanted me out and I wasn't ready to go. I left around midnight, took the 27 bus across town to Doug's place. Doug Halbert. Stayed there until — I don't know, until it was getting light. Took the first bus back. When I came in he was already — he was like that. I didn't do this. I know how it looks. We'd been arguing for months. But I didn't do this. I left at midnight and he was alive.

title: 015 — Halbert — Alibi — Statement
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_015_halbert_alibi_statement
SOURCE_TYPE: witness_statement
AUTHOR: Doug Halbert
RECIPIENT: Det. Mariah Sweeney
TIMESTAMP: 1989-12-18T16:00:00
LOCATION: Ashford Police Station
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.49

TEXT:
Patrick came over after midnight sometime. We had some beers. Put on a movie — the Schwarzenegger one, I forget which. He stayed maybe two hours. Left before the movie was over. I went to bed. No, I don't know what time exactly. Was he upset? Not really. Tired. He's always tired lately. We've been friends since high school. He's not — he wouldn't.

title: 016 — Forrester — Neighbor — Statement
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_016_forrester_neighbor_statement
SOURCE_TYPE: witness_statement
AUTHOR: Annette Forrester (Apt 3B, 412 Wendell)
RECIPIENT: Det. Mariah Sweeney
TIMESTAMP: 1989-12-18T13:15:00
LOCATION: 412 Wendell, building lobby
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.66

TEXT:
They were at it again on the seventeenth. Started around eleven, maybe a little before. Same shouting they've been doing since the autumn — the one wants the other out, the other won't go. I heard the front door of their apartment slam around midnight. I assumed it was Patrick leaving — he's usually the one who storms out. After that I slept until almost four when my cat knocked something off the dresser. Building was quiet then. Nothing else until the police arrived in the morning.

title: 017 — Vong — Letter
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_017_vong_letter
SOURCE_TYPE: private_letter
AUTHOR: Theresa Vong
RECIPIENT: Maya Briggs (mutual friend)
TIMESTAMP: 1989-12-29T22:00:00
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.71

TEXT:
Maya,
I'm writing because I can't stop thinking about it. I saw Eric on Friday the fifteenth, three days before. He told me he was finally going to do it — tell Patrick he had to be out by January first. He said he didn't care if it got ugly. I told him he could have the spare room in my apartment if he needed somewhere to land. He thanked me and said he hoped it wouldn't come to that. Patrick had been — I don't know how to say this without sounding like I'm accusing him, but Patrick had been frightening Eric. Eric had a bruise on his wrist in October and laughed it off. I should have said something.
Tess

title: 018 — Halloran — Autopsy
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_018_halloran_autopsy
SOURCE_TYPE: medical_report
AUTHOR: Dr. Edward Mariani
RECIPIENT: Ashford PD
TIMESTAMP: 1989-12-19T13:00:00
LOCATION: County Morgue
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.88

TEXT:
Subject: Eric Halloran. Cause of death: asphyxiation by ligature strangulation. Ligature: braided electrical cord, consistent with the cord recovered from the broken bedside lamp at the scene. Additional findings: bruising at the throat under the ligature line, including thumb-shaped marks anterolaterally, indicating manual strangulation preceded ligature application. Defensive wounds on both forearms; one bitten fingertip on the left hand (the decedent's own bite, defensive). Toxicology: blood alcohol 0.09. No barbiturates, no opiates, no sedatives. Time of death window 12:30 to 03:00 on 18 December 1989. Pattern is consistent with a struggle of one to several minutes followed by finishing with the ligature. Attacker was within reach of the bedside lamp.

title: 019 — Sweeney — Case — Note
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_019_sweeney_case_note
SOURCE_TYPE: incident_note
AUTHOR: Det. Mariah Sweeney
RECIPIENT: Ashford PD internal file
TIMESTAMP: 1990-01-15T10:00:00
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.79

TEXT:
Internal note re: Halloran. Three interviews with Patrick Vail to date. The general shape of his account is consistent — argument, departed for Halbert's around midnight, returned at dawn — but the timing has shifted on each retelling. First interview placed his return at "around six." Second placed it at "five-thirty or six." Third placed it at "a little before six." Halbert's corroboration is consistent in shape but vague in detail; on second pressing he allowed that Vail "could have left after two." Manner of death and intimacy of attack (manual pressure, defensive wounds, bitten finger) point to a known associate. Absence of sedative and absence of theft are entirely inconsistent with the Reyes/Webb pattern. Recommend continued focus on Vail. Recommend explicit non-linkage to the rail-corridor cases in any internal or external communication.

title: 020 — Cab — Driver — Statement
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_020_cab_driver_statement
SOURCE_TYPE: witness_statement
AUTHOR: Harlan Bowes, taxi driver
RECIPIENT: Det. Hassan Reilly
TIMESTAMP: 1990-11-21T14:00:00
LOCATION: Ashford Police Station
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.57

TEXT:
I'd been parked at the Greyhound terminal waiting for a fare. The kid — I'd seen him around there for a few weeks, sleeping in the doorway some nights — he was sitting on the bench eating from a paper bag. There was a guy with him, standing. The guy was — average. Glasses, the small wire kind. Dark jacket. Soft voice; I couldn't hear them but the kid was nodding along and the guy wasn't gesturing. Could've been a teacher having a chat. I picked up a fare around eleven and when I came back twenty minutes later they were both gone. I didn't think anything of it. The kid would talk to anybody who'd buy him a sandwich.

title: 021 — Hoyts — Deli — Receipt
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_021_hoyts_deli_receipt
SOURCE_TYPE: administrative_record
AUTHOR: Hoyt's Deli register tape
RECIPIENT: Ashford PD (submitted by owner)
TIMESTAMP: 1990-10-04T22:47:00
LOCATION: Hoyt's Deli, 224 Greyhound Pl.
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.81

TEXT:
Register tape, October 4, 1990, 22:47. 1x ham sandwich ($2.25), 1x coffee ($0.50), 1x cigarettes Marlboro ($1.85). Subtotal $4.60. Tax $0.23. Total $4.83. Cash tendered $5.00. Change $0.17. (Hoyt's owner provided this receipt to police when the Marsh missing-persons notice circulated, noting that the customer had ordered the sandwich "for the kid outside.")

title: 022 — Motel — Clerk — Statement
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_022_motel_clerk_statement
SOURCE_TYPE: witness_statement
AUTHOR: Cyril Madoc, night clerk (Roadway Inn)
RECIPIENT: Det. Hassan Reilly
TIMESTAMP: 1990-11-22T09:30:00
LOCATION: Roadway Inn, 410 Greyhound Pl.
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.63

TEXT:
A gentleman came in around eleven-thirty on the fourth, asked for a single, paid cash for one night, signed the book "R. Albers." White, maybe early thirties, glasses — small ones, wire frames — dark jacket. Soft-spoken. The thing I remembered was he asked whether the room had good light. I said the lamp worked fine. He said no, he meant for photography. He said he did portraits sometimes and needed to see his work. At eleven-thirty at night, you know. I gave him Room 14. I never saw who else came in or went out. Wasn't my job to watch the lot.

title: 023 — Marsh — Discovery — Report
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_023_marsh_discovery_report
SOURCE_TYPE: official_report
AUTHOR: Deputy J. Tarrant, Ashford County Sheriff
RECIPIENT: Sheriff's case file / Ashford PD
TIMESTAMP: 1990-11-17T16:30:00
LOCATION: Drainage ditch, Atchison freight depot
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.82

TEXT:
Body recovered from drainage ditch on the southwest perimeter of the disused Atchison freight depot, approximately 600 yards from the active rail siding. Reported by deer hunters who entered the depot grounds and noted unusual scent and corvid activity. Adult white male, decomposition advanced. No identification on body, no wallet. Body subsequently identified via dental records and a small tattoo on the left shoulder ("M") as Tyler Marsh, missing since October 4, 1990. Posture and lividity inconsistent with recovery position; body was moved post-mortem.

title: 024 — Marsh — Coroner — Report
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_024_marsh_coroner_report
SOURCE_TYPE: medical_report
AUTHOR: Dr. Edward Mariani
RECIPIENT: Ashford County Sheriff / Ashford PD
TIMESTAMP: 1990-11-20T10:00:00
LOCATION: County Morgue
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.85

TEXT:
Subject: Tyler Marsh. Cause of death consistent with acute barbiturate intoxication. Decomposition advanced; estimated 5-7 weeks post-mortem at recovery, consistent with a death date in early-to-mid October. No defensive wounds. No ligature. No blunt force trauma. Body moved post-mortem (lividity inconsistent with recovery position). Stomach contents at time of death contained ham, bread, and processed cheese — consistent with the sandwich purchased at Hoyt's Deli on October 4 at 22:47. Manner of death: undetermined; circumstances strongly suggestive of administration by another party.

title: 025 — Group — Home — Record
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_025_group_home_record
SOURCE_TYPE: administrative_record
AUTHOR: Whitlock Group Home, Indianapolis
RECIPIENT: Ashford PD (via interstate inquiry)
TIMESTAMP: 1990-11-22T14:00:00
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.83

TEXT:
Resident: Tyler Marsh, DOB 03/02/1970. Voluntary departure April 11, 1990 at age 20. History: three prior unauthorized absences (1988, 1989) all of short duration. No emergency contacts on file (biological parents deceased; no living relatives located in placement search). Marsh was eligible to leave on his twentieth birthday but chose to remain until April; reasons for April departure not recorded. No subsequent contact with home staff. Filed as low-priority missing person upon expiration of voluntary care window.

title: 026 — Sentinel — Article
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_026_sentinel_article
SOURCE_TYPE: local_news
AUTHOR: K. Wallace, Ashford Daily Sentinel
RECIPIENT: Public
TIMESTAMP: 1990-11-25T07:00:00
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.42

TEXT:
"FOUR DEATHS HAUNT ASHFORD: Pattern Emerges?"
The recovery last week of Tyler Marsh, 20, from the grounds of the abandoned Atchison depot brings to four the number of unexplained deaths in Ashford in just over two years. Marsh joins Daniel Reyes (1988), Marcus Webb (1989), and Eric Halloran (1989) on a list that has begun to draw uncomfortable comparisons. Sources close to the investigation, speaking on condition of anonymity, point to a recurring description: a soft-spoken man in his late twenties or early thirties, wire-rimmed glasses, dark jacket. "If it isn't one person, it's an awful coincidence," one source said. Ashford PD spokesperson declined to comment on whether the cases are being investigated as connected.

title: 027 — Pelham — Clearance
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_027_pelham_clearance
SOURCE_TYPE: official_report
AUTHOR: Det. Hassan Reilly
RECIPIENT: Ashford PD case file
TIMESTAMP: 1989-04-18T15:00:00
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.80

TEXT:
Follow-up on Webb tip ("fifty dollars to pose"): interviewed Larry Pelham, proprietor of Pelham Studio (Sixth Street). Pelham acknowledges occasionally hiring local models for catalog work and bridal portfolios; offers between $50-$100 per session. Provided client logs and tax records. On the date of Webb's disappearance (March 8-9), Pelham was photographing the Lavalle-Stuart wedding in Cincinnati, OH. Alibi corroborated by three members of the wedding party, timestamped Polaroid proof prints, and a Hampton Inn receipt. Cleared as suspect in Webb matter. No basis to connect to Reyes matter. Case file closed re: Pelham.

title: 028 — Kell — Witness — Statement
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_028_kell_witness_statement
SOURCE_TYPE: witness_statement
AUTHOR: Frances Kell (resident, 410 Wendell)
RECIPIENT: Det. Mariah Sweeney
TIMESTAMP: 1990-01-04T11:30:00
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.38

TEXT:
After I read about the Halloran boy in the paper I remembered: I saw a man on Wendell that night. Tall. He had glasses. Walking along the sidewalk on the west side. It was the night of the seventeenth because I'd been at bingo and was walking back. He was just walking. I couldn't see his face. He had a dark coat or a jacket. He was tall. That's all I remember. Yes, the seventeenth, I'm sure. I always go to bingo on Tuesdays.
[Internal annotation, MS: St. Anselm's bingo records show Mrs. Kell present on December 12 and 14 of that week. The 17th was a Sunday; no bingo was held. Witness reliability noted as low.]

title: 029 — Pawnshop — Record
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_029_pawnshop_record
SOURCE_TYPE: administrative_record
AUTHOR: Eli Hennessey, proprietor (Hennessey Pawn)
RECIPIENT: Ashford PD
TIMESTAMP: 1988-07-20T15:15:00
LOCATION: Hennessey Pawn, 88 Fourth St.
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.77

TEXT:
Ledger entry, July 20, 1988. Item: Polaroid 600 Instant Camera, used, with strap. Sale to shop (not pawn): $18.00. Seller name as recorded: "James Conroy." ID presented: Ohio driver's license (photocopy taken). Seller description in margin: "tall, glasses, dark jacket, polite." (Note from Hennessey, 1991: when contacted about this entry following the Sentinel article, I checked the file. The photocopy of the Conroy license was lost in the office flood in spring 1991. I do recall the man — quiet, didn't haggle. The address on the license, when checked at the time, turned out to be a vacant lot in Toledo.)

title: 030 — County — Fair — Bulletin
text:
SOURCE_ID: source_030_county_fair_bulletin
SOURCE_TYPE: market_notice
AUTHOR: Ashford County Fair Board
RECIPIENT: Public
TIMESTAMP: 1990-09-28T08:00:00
LOCATION: County Courthouse bulletin board
RELIABILITY_PRIOR: 0.92

TEXT:
The 47th Annual Ashford County Fair will be held October 12-15, 1990 at the fairgrounds on Route 7. Livestock auction Saturday at 10 a.m. The carnival midway returns this year after a one-year absence. Volunteer signups for ticket booth and parking are available at the courthouse, Room 104. Junior division entries (livestock, baking, and crafts) must be submitted by October 5. Adult division entries due October 8.
